title: Inconsistent behaviour of normal and prepared statement when use values with different types in CASE WHEN
description:
### What happens?

Hi, in the following test case, the normal SELECT is equivalent to the prepared one, however, the normal SELECT triggers an error:

```sql
CREATE TABLE t97(c0 DOUBLE);
INSERT INTO t97(c0) VALUES (0.7430236362015866);
SELECT (CASE t97.c0 WHEN t97.c0 THEN t97.c0 ELSE '' END ) FROM t97 GROUP BY '', t97.c0; -- Binder Error: Cannot mix values of type VARCHAR and DOUBLE in CASE expression - an explicit cast is required
PREPARE prepare_query AS SELECT (CASE t97.c0 WHEN t97.c0 THEN t97.c0 ELSE ? END ) FROM t97 GROUP BY '', t97.c0;
EXECUTE prepare_query(''); -- 0.7430236362015866
```

Furthermore, if I remove the GROUP BY in the normal SELECT, it will not trigger any error.

```sql
CREATE TABLE t97(c0 DOUBLE);
INSERT INTO t97(c0) VALUES (0.7430236362015866);
SELECT (CASE t97.c0 WHEN t97.c0 THEN t97.c0 ELSE '' END ) FROM t97; -- 0.7430236362015866
```

### To Reproduce

```sql
CREATE TABLE t97(c0 DOUBLE);
INSERT INTO t97(c0) VALUES (0.7430236362015866);
SELECT (CASE t97.c0 WHEN t97.c0 THEN t97.c0 ELSE '' END ) FROM t97 GROUP BY '', t97.c0;
PREPARE prepare_query AS SELECT (CASE t97.c0 WHEN t97.c0 THEN t97.c0 ELSE ? END ) FROM t97 GROUP BY '', t97.c0;
EXECUTE prepare_query('');

SELECT (CASE t97.c0 WHEN t97.c0 THEN t97.c0 ELSE '' END ) FROM t97;
```

### OS:

Ubuntu 24.04

### DuckDB Version:

4b7a6b7bd0f8c968bfecab08e801cdc1f0a5cdfd

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

PREPARE prepare_query FROM 'SELECT (CASE WHEN t98.c0 < 2 THEN ? ELSE t98.c0 END) FROM t98 GROUP BY t98.c1, t98.c0;
key_bug_features: CASE WHEN with mixed types in GROUP BY clause
